title: Matthew Kraemer
content:
Partner | Tax Group Regional Practice Lead - Calgary
People.ContactEmail mkraemer@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2009
- University of Calgary
Recognized for his detail-oriented and pragmatic approach, Matthew helps clients seamlessly navigate the corporate tax planning and tax disputes landscape
A partner in our National Tax Group in Calgary, Matthew is a trusted advisor to clients across Canada on complex, high-stakes tax matters. He provides strategic counsel on corporate tax planning, domestic and cross-border reorganizations, mergers and acquisitions, and tax litigation, guiding clients through transactions and disputes that are critical to their business success.
Matthew is valued for his ability to quickly grasp the commercial realities behind every mandate. He takes the time to understand each client’s operations, risk tolerance, and long-term objectives, enabling him to craft innovative, tax-efficient structures that are both technically sound and commercially practical. He advises public and private corporations across a broad range of industries that drive Western Canada’s economy, including Energy & Infrastructure, Oil & Gas, Resources, Technology, and Real Estate. His deep industry knowledge allows him to anticipate challenges, navigate regulatory complexity, and deliver tailored solutions to novel and highly technical issues.
A steadfast advocate, Matthew represents clients in contentious tax matters with focus and precision. In disputes with tax authorities, including the Canada Revenue Agency, he leverages his thorough understanding of statutory frameworks and compliance requirements to achieve favourable outcomes. He advises on audit strategy and conduct, supports proactive planning to mitigate reassessment risk, and advances clients’ positions through objections and judicial appeals when necessary.
Matthew has served as a sessional instructor at the University of Calgary Faculty of Law, where he taught Basic Tax and International Tax. He is a member of the Law Society of Alberta, the Law Society of British Columbia, the Canadian Bar Association, the Canadian Tax Foundation, and the Canadian Petroleum Tax Society. He also served as past Chair of Big Brothers Big Sisters of Calgary and Area, reflecting his commitment to community leadership.

#### Publications & Presentations
- Author, Tax Litigation Journal, "Revisiting the Tax Benefit Component of the GAAR", 2021, Federated Press, Volume XXIV, No.1
- Sessional Instructor, International Tax, University of Calgary, 2020 - 2021

- Presenter, “Mobility and Executive Compensation”, Canadian Tax Foundation, 2018 National Conference
- Presenter, “Successor Rules and Other Considerations for Purchasers and Vendors”, 2016 CPTS National Conference
